tool_dispute.name: The Tool Dispute
tool_dispute.description: Two settlers are arguing over the last good wrench.

steps.prompt: Read the room. What's really going on?
steps.success_text: You see it now — this isn't about the wrench. {npc_name} watches you, impressed.
steps.failure_text: You try to read the situation, but the tension is too thick.

steps.success_text: Your words cut through the anger. Both settlers unclench.
steps.failure_text: They're too wound up to listen right now.

steps.prompt: Maybe you can make a second tool.
steps.success_text: You cobble together a workable replacement from scrap.

resolution.full_success: Both settlers thank you. The argument is forgotten.
resolution.partial_success: Not perfect, but the argument is over. For now.

homesick_talk.name: Homesick
homesick_talk.description: {npc_name} is staring at the void with a faraway look.
homesick_talk.success_text: You sit with {npc_name} and listen. Sometimes that's all it takes.
homesick_talk.failure_text: You try, but the words come out wrong. {npc_name} looks away.

skeptic_debate.name: Prove Your Worth
skeptic_debate.description: Dax wants proof the skerry plan isn't doomed.

tactics.name: Appeal to Evidence
tactics.flavor: You lay out the facts — materials gathered, rooms built, people fed.

tactics.name: Appeal to Emotion
tactics.flavor: You speak to what she's really afraid of — being wrong about trusting someone.

tactics.name: Show, Don't Tell
tactics.flavor: You pick up the nearest broken thing and fix it.

skeptic_debate.win_text: Dax nods once. 'Fine. You might not be an idiot.'
skeptic_debate.lose_text: Dax turns away. 'Come back when you have better answers.'

lonely_night.name: Can't Sleep
lonely_night.description: Chris is sitting alone in the dark, knees drawn up.
lonely_night.success_text: You sit down next to him. No words needed. After a while, his shoulders stop shaking.
lonely_night.failure_text: You try to help, but you say the wrong thing. Chris curls up tighter.

scholar_puzzle.name: Ancient Inscription
scholar_puzzle.description: Callum found an inscription on a piece of salvage. He needs help deciphering it.

steps.success_text: You spot a pattern Callum missed — the symbols repeat in threes.
steps.failure_text: The markings blur together. You can't make sense of them.

steps.success_text: You calm him down. He refocuses and sees it clearly now.
steps.failure_text: Callum mutters something about amateurs and turns away.

resolution.full_success: Together, you crack the inscription. Callum is ecstatic.
resolution.partial_success: You got partway there. Callum will work out the rest.
resolution.failure: The inscription remains a mystery. Callum is frustrated.

garden_crisis.name: Wilting Garden
garden_crisis.description: Something is wrong with the garden plots. Plants are wilting fast.

steps.success_text: You find it — a crack in the soil bed is draining nutrients.
steps.failure_text: Everything looks the same. You can't pinpoint the problem.

steps.prompt: Repair the damage before more plants die.
steps.success_text: You patch the crack and redirect the nutrient flow. The plants perk up.
steps.failure_text: Your patch doesn't hold. More soil leaks through.

steps.prompt: Reassure the settlers who depend on this food.
steps.success_text: Your words steady everyone. They roll up their sleeves to help.
steps.failure_text: People look worried. The food situation is getting tense.

resolution.full_success: The garden is saved. Everyone breathes easier.
resolution.partial_success: Some damage, but the garden will recover.
resolution.failure: The garden is in rough shape. People are anxious.
